items.mistake: Using the smallest/fastest model for complex reasoning or long documents.
items.fix: Match model tier to task difficulty and context length; use higher-capability models for multi-step analysis.
items.why: Under-powered models truncate context or produce shallow answers that look confident.
items.example: Feeding a 200-page contract to a Haiku-class call without chunking yields missed clauses.

items.mistake: Expecting vision models to read tiny text or low-resolution scans perfectly.
items.fix: Provide clear crops, higher resolution, or OCR text alongside images for critical fields.
items.why: Vision is helpful but not infallible on blurry photos or dense tables.
items.example: A photo of a receipt with glare may misread totals—paste the numbers as text for expense approval.
